info.title: Basis Platform API
info.description:
# Introduction

The Basis API is designed to be easy to use and simple to test to help customers
and partners get up and running quickly. Use the API endpoints to connect to
Basis to retrieve campaign planning and delivery data. The current version of
the API is v1.

# Getting Started

The Basis API is a RESTful API. This means that the API is designed to allow you
to work with objects using familiar HTTP verbs.

* read = GET
* create = POST
* update = PUT
* delete = DELETE

All production API requests are made to [https://api.basis.net/v1](https://api.basis.net/v1).
There is also a testing sandbox to use when developing and testing applications,
with requests being made to [https://api-sandbox.basis.net/v1](https://api-sandbox.basis.net/v1).
The Basis API is available to Centro customers and integration partners, and all
organizations must acquire valid credentials prior to using the web services.
Contact your Centro representative to obtain valid credentials.

#### API Request Limits

To maintain optimum performance and ensure that the Basis Platform API is available to all our customers,
Basis balances transaction loads by imposing rate limits of 75,000 requests per hour across all endpoints per API user.

Generating client credentials tokens is also limited to 10 per hour, with a maximum of 25 per day. More information
on this can be found in the Client Credentials Flow section below.

# Authentication

All API endpoints require authentication.  Basis API uses [OAuth2](https://oauth.net/2/). Supported grant types:

* [Authorization Code](https://auth0.com/docs/get-started/authentication-and-authorization-flow/authorization-code-flow)
* [Client Credentials](https://auth0.com/docs/get-started/authentication-and-authorization-flow/client-credentials-flow)
* [Refresh Token](https://auth0.com/docs/secure/tokens/refresh-tokens/use-refresh-tokens)

The password grant flow has now been deprecated. Please update your applications to use one of the above two flows.

If you encounter any issues or challenges while setting up the OAuth connection, please don't hesitate to contact us
for support.

Please note that OAuth authentication uses `https://auth.basis.net` not `https://api.basis.net`.

#### Authorization Code Flow

To authorize your API access, visit the following URL in your browser, replacing
placeholders with your specific values as needed:

`https://auth.basis.net/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=<client_id>&scope=openid profile email offline_access&state=<your state token>&redirect_uri=<redirect URI>&audience=https://api.basis.net`

After successful authorization you will be redirected to your specified redirect
URI and the authorization code as well as the `state` (more info
[here](https://www.oauth.com/oauth2-servers/getting-ready/)) supplied in the
request will be passed as parameters.

Once you have this auth code you will need to make a second request to get a token which
can be used to make API requests:

```
curl -X POST https://auth.basis.net/oauth/token
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data-urlencode 'grant_type=authorization_code' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_id=<your application ID>' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_secret=<your application secret>' \
  --data-urlencode 'code=<authorization code>' \
  --data-urlencode 'redirect_uri=<your application redirect URI>'
```

This token can then be sent along with API requests in the Authorization header as
outlined in the next section. If you want a refresh token as well, make sure to include
the `offline_access` scope in the /authorize request above.

Note: In order to use this flow successfully, your application needs to have an associated
redirect URI. If you did not previously have a redirect URI set and need to set one, reach
out to Basis support.

#### Client Credentials Flow

```
curl --location 'https://auth.basis.net/oauth/token' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data-urlencode 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_id=<your application ID>' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_secret=<your application secret>' \
  --data-urlencode 'audience=https://api.basis.net'
```

A few important notes:
* Your application must have an associated owner ID (i.e. agency ID) in order to use this flow. 
  It will return a 401 otherwise. If you did not previously have an owner ID set and need to set
  one, reach out to Basis support.
* This token returns all clients for an agency, so if there are restricted clients, we recommend
  using the authorization code flow above.
* Endpoints providing user information will return a 404, since this token is associated with
  an organization, not a user.

##### Client Credentials Token Quota

Client credentials token generation is limited to **10 per hour**, with a maximum of **25 per day**.
If you exceed this limit, you'll receive a `429 Too Many Requests` error. Please reach out to
Basis Support if you need to increase this limit.

If you receive this 429 error, you can check the headers of the response for real-time information
on your current usage and limits. See the
[official Auth0 docs](https://auth0.com/docs/fine-grained-m2m-token-quotas-early-access#monitor-token-quota-consumption)
for more information.

#### Refresh Token Flow
```
curl --location 'https://auth.basis.net/oauth/token' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data-urlencode 'grant_type=refresh_token' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_id=<your application ID>' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_secret=<your application secret>' \
  --data-urlencode 'refresh_token=<your refresh token>' \
```

A few important notes:
* Only the authorization code flow returns a refresh token.
* The refresh token can be used to obtain a new access token when the current one expires.
* The refresh token flow will itself return a refresh token.

#### Request Data

Once you have your access token from the token response you can make requests to
the API with header `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`.

For example:

```
curl -X GET "https://api.basis.net/v1/agency" -H "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>"
```

To try the endpoints within this documentation, click **Authorize** below, and
use a token obtained via any of the above methods to fill in the "Bearer" field.

# Analytics API

The analytics API gives you access to delivery statistics and details of your
Basis resources:

* agency
* clients
* brands
* campaigns
* line items
* groups
* tactics
* add-ons
* vendors
* properties
* verticals
* key performance indicators (KPI)

## Statistics

Retrieve statistics by calling

`GET /v1/stats/{scope}`

Use the scope parameter to choose how the statistics are aggregated:

* line_item
* daily_by_line_item
* daily

A line item describes the details of a media purchase or programmatic buy.
Statistics returned for each line item include delivery metrics, such as
impressions, clicks, and video completions, and performance metrics like eCPM,
eCPC, and eCPV.

## Finding Resource Details

Many of the Analytics API endpoints list all of a particular type of resource, like

`GET /v1/brands`

which returns a list of all of your brands, as well as the ID, name, creation
timestamp, and verticals for each.

Many other endpoints return details for one resource, like

`GET /v1/brands/{id}`

which returns the ID, name, creation timestamp, and verticals for the brand with
the given brand ID.

### Resource Relationships

Campaigns have

* 1 client
* 1 brand
* many line items
* many add-ons

Line items have

* 1 campaign
* 1 property
* 1 vendor

Campaign details returned by `GET /v1/campaigns/{id}` or `GET /v1/campaigns`
include the client_id and brand_id for the campaign. To find the line items for
a campaign, call `GET /v1/campaigns/{id}/line_items`.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated user
Returns details for the agency, the organization representing a buyer.
Returns details for one brand.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single brand.

created_at.description: The date and time the vertical was added.
The date and time the sub-vertical was added.

get.description: Lists all brands.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to paginate through the list of records. Don't set this parameter to see the first page of records, then check the metadata object in the response for the cursor to use to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Used to refine the results by a chosen parameter. Valid parameters = name

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single client. Set this parameter to limit the brands to the specified client.

cursor.description: Use this cursor value in your next call to this endpoint to return the next set of items

created_at.description: The date and time the vertical was added.
The date and time the sub-vertical was added.

get.description: Returns details for one client. An agency can represent multiple clients, and each client can have multiple brands.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single client.

get.description: Lists all clients.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to paginate through the list of records. Don't set this parameter to see the first page of records, then check the metadata object in the response for the cursor to use to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Used to refine the results by a chosen parameter. Valid parameters are name, contact_first_name, contact_last_name, contact_email, billing_name, notes

cursor.description: Use this cursor value in your next call to this endpoint to return the next set of items

get.description: Returns details for one vendor, an entity or organization selling and offering products, content, or services for payment. Examples of vendors would include the owners of websites that offer advertisement space and exchanges that offers ad space on multiple websites.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single vendor.

get.description: Lists all vendors, entities or organizations selling and offering products, content, or services for payment. Examples of vendors would include the owners of websites that offer advertisement space and exchanges that offers ad space on multiple websites.

get.description: Returns details for a single line item.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single campaign.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single line item.

name.description: The name of the line item.

start_date.description: The first day impressions can be delivered for the line item.

end_date.description: The last day impressions can be delivered for the line item.

ad_sizes.description: An array of eligible creative dimensions.

formats.description: An array of eligible creative formats (audio, display, interstitial, video).

platforms.description: An array of eligible platforms (web browser, mobile, tablet).

rate_type.description: Determines how cost is measured for the line item (CPM, CPC, flat, etc.).

amount_gain_loss_contracted.description: The agreed on gain or loss, gross cost - media cost - ad serving cost.

media_contracted_units.description: The agreed-on units (impressions, clicks, video plays, etc.) to be delivered.

media_spend_contracted.description: The agreed-on amount to spend on media.

ad_serving_rate_type.description: Determines how ad serving costs are is measured.

kpi_id.description: Identifies a single Key Performance Indicator (KPI).

kpi_goal.description: The goal set for the Key Performance Indicator (KPI).

advertising_channel.description: Identifies the advertising channel a Planning User associated to the line item.

404.description: Campaign or line item not found

schema.description: Campaign or line item not found

get.description: Returns details for a single Group.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single group id. In the absence of this id, all groups that this user has access to should be returned.

name.description: The display name for the requested group.

budget.description: The budget settings for this group.

flight_dates.description: The specific dates under which this group will run.

from.description: Start date of the tactics within this group's flight.

to.description: End date of the tactics within this group's flight.

even_delivery_enabled.description: Set to True if the budget amount should be evenly delivered throughout the day.

type.description: Budget type. Possible values are: all_time, daily

status.description: The tactic's status controls whether campaigns in the group can bid on impressions.

pacing_setting.description: Controls whether budget pacing is controlled at the group or tactic level.

get.description: Lists all Groups.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to paginate through the list of records. Don't set this parameter to see the first page of records, then check the metadata object in the response for the cursor to use to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Used to refine the results by a chosen parameter. Valid parameter = name

cursor.description: Use this cursor value in your next call to this endpoint to return the next set of items

name.description: The display name for the requested group.

budget.description: The budget settings for this group.

flight_dates.description: The specific dates under which this group will run.

from.description: Start date of the tactics within this group's flight.

to.description: End date of the tactics within this group's flight.

even_delivery_enabled.description: Set to True if the budget amount should be evenly delivered throughout the day.

type.description: Budget type. Possible values are: all_time, daily

status.description: The tactic's status controls whether campaigns in the group can bid on impressions.

pacing_setting.description: Controls whether budget pacing is controlled at the group or tactic level.

get.description: Returns details for a single Tactic.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Identifies a single tactic id. In the absence of this id, all tactics that this user has access to should be returned.

name.description: The display name for the requested tactic.

group_id.description: Identifies the single group ID that is a parent of the requested tactic.

budget.description: The budget settings for this tactic.

amount.description: Amount in dollars budgeted for this tactic.

schedule.description: The budget scheduling settings applied to this tactic.

flight_dates.description: The specific dates under which this tactic will run.

type.description: Type of budget used for this tactic. Possible values are: all_time, daily

even_delivery_enabled.description: Set to True if the budget amount should be evenly delivered throughout the day.

pacing_priority.description: How should this tactic be prioritized compared to your other tactics? Default value is 1.

status.description: The current status of this tactic. Possible values are: online, offline, archived

default_bid.description: The default bid set for this tactic.

get.description: Lists all Tactics.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to paginate through the list of records. Don't set this parameter to see the first page of records, then check the metadata object in the response for the cursor to use to fetch the next page.

parameters.description: Used to refine the results by a chosen parameter. Valid parameter = name

group_id.description: Identifies the single group ID that is a parent of the requested tactic.

budget.description: The budget settings for this tactic.

amount.description: Amount in dollars budgeted for this tactic.

schedule.description: The budget scheduling settings applied to this tactic.

flight_dates.description: The specific dates under which this tactic will run.

type.description: Type of budget used for this tactic. Possible values are: all_time, daily

even_delivery_enabled.description: Set to True if the budget amount should be evenly delivered throughout the day.

pacing_priority.description: How should this tactic be prioritized compared to your other tactics? Default value is 1.

status.description: The current status of this tactic. Possible values are: online, offline, archived

default_bid.description: The default bid set for this tactic.

get.description: Retrieves statistics.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Used to paginate through the list of records. Don't set this parameter to see the first page of records, then check the metadata object in the response for the cursor to use to fetch the next page.

parameters.name: brand_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single brand. Set this parameter to limit the statistics to the specified brand.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single client. Set this parameter to limit the statistics to the specified client.

parameters.name: campaign_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single campaign. Set this parameter to limit the statistics to the specified campaign.

parameters.name: line_item_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single line item. Set this parameter to limit the statistics to the specified line item.

parameters.name: line_item_lineage_id
parameters.description: Identifies a single line item across revisions.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: The earliest date of statistics collection. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: The last date of statistics collection. Format: yyyy-mm-dd

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description:
Defines the granularity and grouping of the statistics to return.
`line_item:` Statistics show totals for each line item for all days in the date range.
`daily_by_line_item:` Statistics show totals for each day in the date range for each line item.
`daily:` Statistics show totals for each day in the date range for each creative.
`daily_by_conversion:` Statistics show a granular breakout of conversion metrics for each day in the date range.

cursor.description: Use this cursor value in your next call to this endpoint to return the next set of items

line_item_id.description:
Present for all scopes.
Identifies a single line item.

line_item_lineage_id.description: Identifies a single line item across revisions.

delivery_source_id.description:
Present for daily and daily_by_conversion scopes.
Identifies a single delivery source.

creative_id.description:
Present for daily and daily_by_conversion scopes.
Identifies a single creative.

external_ad_ref.description:
Present for daily and daily_by_conversion scopes.
Identifies a single ad on the external source.

conversion_id.description:
Present for daily_by_conversion scope.
Identifies a single conversion.

delivered_date.description:
Present for daily_by_line_item and daily_by_conversion scopes.
The date the delivery took place.

actual_start_date.description:
Present for line_item scope.
First day Basis received delivery greater than zero for any delivery metric within the contracted flight range.

data_through_date.description:
Present for line_item scope.
Date stamp of the last day of delivery.

delivery_metrics.description: Present for line_item, daily_by_line_item, and daily scopes.

delivered_units.description: Impressions, clicks, views, or actions delivered. The definition of a unit depends on the line item's chosen rate type.

delivered_impressions.description: Impressions delivered, rounded to a whole number.

delivered_eligible_impressions.description: Impressions delivered against which Basis can measure viewability.

delivered_measurable_impressions.description: Impressions delivered where our viewability provider's script was able to determine if they were viewable or not.

delivered_viewable_impressions.description: Delivered impressions that our viewability provider's script was able to deem viewable.

delivered_interactions.description: Delivered impressions that were expanded or engaged.

delivered_video_starts.description: The number of times a video creative was started.

delivered_video_completions.description: Video impressions that completed (reached the fourth quartile marker).

delivered_total_view_conversions.description: View-through conversions, recorded after a user saw an ad from the campaign but did not click through.

delivered_total_click_conversions.description: Click-through conversions, recorded after a user clicked an ad in the line item.

delivered_total_conversions.description: All click-through and view-through conversions added together.

delivered_cpa_view_conversions.description: Cost per action for view-through conversions.

delivered_cpa_click_conversions.description: Cost per action for click-through conversions.

delivered_cpa_total_conversions.description: Cost per action for total conversions (click-through and view-through combined).

delivered_inventory_spend.description: Dollar amount spent on media inventory only.

delivered_data_spend.description: Dollar amount spent on data services used to serve media inventory.

total_spend.description:
Present for line_item and daily_by_line_item scopes.
Comprehensive amount of purchases based on billable delivery.

media_spend.description:
Present for line_item and daily_by_line_item scopes.
Dollar amount spent on media.

ad_serving_spend.description:
Present for line_item and daily_by_line_item scopes.
In Basis, Ad Serving is a specified ad server rate and calculated cost.

ecpm.description: Effective cost per mille (CPM), the average cost per 1000 impressions.

ecpcv.description: Effective cost per completed view (applies to video only).

ecpv.description: Effective cost per view (applies to video only).

to_kpi_goal_pct.description: Percentage to track the progress of the KPI Goal.

pacing_pct_spend.description: Pacing on-schedule indicator, expressed as a percentage of the expected or desired spend to date.

pacing_pct_units.description: Pacing on-schedule indicator, expressed as a percentage of the expected or desired units delivered to date.

projected_balance.description: The balance projected if the line item continues delivering at the current pace.

total_unspent.description: The amount budgeted that hasn't been spent.

delivery_spend_pct.description: The percent of the budgeted gross that has been spent on delivery.

target_spend.description: The amount budgeted to be spent by this line item.

target_units.description: The number of units to be delivered by this line item.

click_conversion_revenue.description: Total revenue associated with click-through conversions

view_conversion_revenue.description: Total revenue associated with view-through conversions
